prompt: A gritty photorealistic documentary-style street photograph inside a dim, wet Chinese produce market at night, shot at eye level with a shallow depth of field and natural ambient lighting. The foreground right shows a middle-aged Chinese vegetable seller wearing a black T-shirt and red checkered apron, standing behind a vegetable stall and packing long green beans into a clear plastic bag on a digital weighing scale with glowing red numbers. The vendor’s face is intentionally obscured by a soft rectangular blur. In the foreground and midground, display exactly 9 visible produce groups: long green onions/scallions in a white foam tray, pale leafy greens piled high, ginger roots in a small pile, red chili peppers in a bag, long green beans in plastic bags on the scale, cucumbers or dark green leafy vegetables stacked behind, round pale-green cabbages or gourds at right, glossy dark purple eggplants in a white crate, and a large wet fish or seafood tank edge at the bottom right. On the left side, include a blurred man in dark clothing pushing a metal handcart through the wet aisle, also with his face obscured. The aisle floor is glossy and flooded with puddles, reflecting overhead lamps and market lights. The background is heavily blurred with stalls, hanging lamps, Chinese shop signage, indistinct shoppers, tarps, and rows of vegetables receding into depth. Use moody warm fluorescent market lighting, muted greens and browns, high ISO grain, slight motion blur, imperfect focus, damp atmosphere, and unsettling hyperreal realism. Add one large green Chinese sign in the upper left with partially readable characters resembling a vegetable market district sign, but keep it naturally blurred. No clean studio look, no cartoon styling, no visible watermark, no readable brand logos.
